analysis_result: NF2 mutation frequency in the benign NF oncoprint (combined Neurofibroma + Atypical_NF with WES) is approximately 14% (5/36 = 13.89%). Stratified: Neurofibroma alone 18.75% (3/16); Atypical_NF alone 10.00% (2/20).
denominator_used: N = 36 samples with WES data and Tumor Type in {Neurofibroma (n=16), Atypical_NF (n=20)} from MOESM4. 32 of these 36 samples had at least one reported mutation in MOESM5; the remaining 4 had no mutations reported (still counted as not-NF2-mutated, so included in denominator).
assumptions_made: Benign NF interpreted as Neurofibroma + Atypical_NF (per approach stating 'benign/atypical NF'); non-synonymous variants defined as Missense_Mutation, Nonsense_Mutation, Frame_Shift_Del, Frame_Shift_Ins, In_Frame_Del, In_Frame_Ins, Splice_Site, Translation_Start_Site, Nonstop_Mutation (i.e., excluding Silent); Tumor_Sample_Barcode in MOESM5 matched by format 'Tumor_<WES label>' from MOESM4; samples absent from MOESM5 (n=4) assumed to have no WES mutations reported and counted in the denominator but not the numerator; no filtering on variant FILTER/VAF/PASS status was applied beyond the default table content.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - MOESM4 (Clinical Data): 108 rows, 15 columns. Columns used: Tumor_ID, Tumor Type, WES, WES label.
   - MOESM5 (WES mutations): 7457 rows, 123 columns. Columns used: Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Hugo_Symbol, Variant_Classification.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter 1 (MOESM4): WES == 'X' -> 54 samples.
   - Filter 2: Tumor Type in {'Neurofibroma', 'Atypical_NF'} -> 36 samples (16 Neurofibroma + 20 Atypical_NF).
   - Built expected MOESM5 barcodes as 'Tumor_' + WES label.
   - 32 of these 36 samples appeared in MOESM5 (had >=1 mutation reported); 4 had no mutations in MOESM5.

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Gene: Hugo_Symbol == 'NF2'.
   - Non-synonymous Variant_Classification set: {Missense_Mutation, Nonsense_Mutation, Frame_Shift_Del, Frame_Shift_Ins, In_Frame_Del, In_Frame_Ins, Splice_Site, Translation_Start_Site, Nonstop_Mutation} (excludes Silent).
   - Sample match: Tumor_Sample_Barcode format 'Tumor_<WES label>'.

D. COMPUTATION
   - NF2 non-synonymous mutation events in cohort: 5 mutations across 5 unique samples.
     - Tumor_1543: NF2 Splice_Site p.X579_splice
     - Tumor_1682: NF2 Nonsense_Mutation p.Q470*
     - Tumor_1856: NF2 Missense_Mutation p.R160Q
     - Tumor_1890: NF2 In_Frame_Del p.K20_I26del
     - Tumor_2179: NF2 Nonsense_Mutation p.R341*
   - Numerator: 5 samples with >=1 NF2 non-synonymous mutation.
   - Denominator: 36 benign/atypical NF WES samples.
   - Frequency: 5/36 = 13.89%.

E. RESULT
   - Combined benign NF (Neurofibroma + Atypical_NF): NF2 mutation frequency = 5/36 = 13.89% (~14%).
   - Stratified: Neurofibroma 3/16 = 18.75%; Atypical_NF 2/20 = 10.00%.

F. CAVEATS
   - 'Benign NF oncoprint' in Supplementary Figure 3a may correspond to only Neurofibroma (without atypical), only Atypical_NF, or a combined panel; the approach specified 'benign/atypical NF' which I interpreted as the combined set (most inclusive reading). If the oncoprint is Neurofibroma-only the frequency would be ~19%; if Atypical_NF-only it would be 10%.
   - 4 samples in the benign/atypical cohort have no mutations in MOESM5; they are retained in the denominator (standard oncoprint convention).
   - No additional VAF/FILTER thresholds were applied; all listed variants were used as-is.
   - Variant_Classification list used is a standard MAF-style non-synonymous classifier set; paper may have used a slightly different definition.
